title: Half-life
subtitle: The time for activity to halve

steps.title: Quick question first…
steps.question: After two half-lives, how much of a radioactive sample remains?
steps.misconceptionFeedback: ⚠️ Common misconception! After one half-life, half remains. After two half-lives, half of that half remains — so only a quarter (25%) is left. The sample never reaches exactly zero.
steps.correctFeedback: ✓ Correct! Each half-life halves the remaining amount: after 2 half-lives, (1/2) × (1/2) = 1/4 remains.
steps.bestSource: BEST resource: AQA Physics specification 4.4.4 — Half-life

steps.title: Half-life
steps.bodyHTML: The half-life of a radioactive isotope is the time taken for the activity (or number of undecayed nuclei) to fall to half its initial value. After n half-lives, the fraction of nuclei remaining is (1/2)ⁿ, so the percentage remaining = 100 × (0.5)ⁿ. Because decay is random, the half-life is a statistical property of a large number of nuclei — individual atoms decay unpredictably.

relationships: Each additional half-life halves the remaining amount
The sample approaches zero but theoretically never reaches it

commonErrors: Thinking the sample fully decays after a set number of half-lives
Adding half-lives instead of multiplying fractions
Confusing activity (decays per second) with the number of remaining nuclei

steps.title: See it in action
steps.instruction: Drag the slider to set the number of half-lives. Watch how the percentage of nuclei remaining drops with each step.

aqa.questionText: A radioactive isotope has a half-life of 5 days. A sample initially contains 1600 undecayed nuclei. Calculate the number of undecayed nuclei remaining after 15 days.

steps.label: Find the number of half-lives
steps.content: Divide total time by the half-life:

steps.label: Apply the half-life formula
steps.content: Each half-life halves the number of nuclei:
steps.markSchemeNote: 1 mark — correct use of (0.5)ⁿ; 1 mark — correct answer

aqa.questionText: A radioactive source has an initial activity of 800 Bq and a half-life of 4 hours. What is its activity after 12 hours?
aqa.markSchemeAward: 1 mark — correct number of half-lives · 1 mark — correct answer 100 Bq
aqa.commonMistake: Option A (400 Bq) is after only 1 half-life. Always find n first before halving repeatedly.
